channel.title: redis on Hengist
channel.description: Recent content in redis on Hengist

item.title: Redis and Memcached
item.description: Main difference Redis Memcached in-memory (except OOM or AOF/RDB enabled) data store and it is not volatile in-memory cache and it is volatile has data types no data types keys with a maximum size of 512MB and also values up to 512MB keys with a maximum size of 250B and values up to 1MB How Redis achieves persistence Redis supports persistence, thus it’s called a data store, in two different ways:
